source: USDA Cooperative Extension (Cornell, WSU, UC ANR, UF/IFAS); USDA-ARS Three Sisters and allelopathy research; Xerces Society pollinator publications; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions; SARE Cover Crops field guide. Strength grades curated honestly: strong = empirical research support, moderate = widely corroborated traditional + some research. Folk-only (weak) entries excluded; community PRs welcome.

entries.description: Beans fix atmospheric nitrogen via Rhizobium root-nodule symbiosis; nitrogen released to companion corn over the season enriches the soil heavy-feeding corn depletes. The defining N-cycle pairing of the Three Sisters.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Corn stalks serve as a living trellis for climbing bean vines, eliminating the need for separate trellising. Plant beans after corn reaches ~6 inches so vines don't outpace the support.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Sprawling squash vines and large leaves shade the soil at the base of the corn, retain moisture, and suppress weeds. The third sister's job is the ground layer.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Squash leaves shade the base of bean plants and conserve moisture; spiny varieties (Hubbard, Buttercup) also help deter raccoons and other corn-bed raiders.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Tall corn provides afternoon shade to young squash vines, reducing transplant shock and heat stress in high-sun regions; especially valuable in arid and continental climates.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Nitrogen released by bean root-nodule activity supplements squash needs as developing fruit ripens.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Basil's aromatic volatile oils repel aphids, whiteflies, and tomato hornworm moths searching for egg-laying sites. The widely-claimed flavor-enhancement effect lacks formal study but is reported across traditional sources.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Tomato canopy provides afternoon shade that protects basil from leaf scorch and bolting in hot summer climates; useful pairing in mediterranean and arid beds.
entries.source: UC ANR Master Gardener publications

entries.description: French marigolds (Tagetes patula) produce alpha-terthienyl and other thiophene compounds in their roots that suppress root-knot nematodes (Meloidogyne spp.). The strongest empirically-supported companion-planting effect known. Plant 12-18 inches from tomato roots.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Signet marigolds (Tagetes tenuifolia) repel whitefly and aphids and provide some nematode suppression, though less than French marigolds. Edible flowers double as garnish.
entries.source: UC ANR Master Gardener publications

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts aphids and whiteflies away from tomato plants as a sacrificial host. Spray nasturtium with insecticidal soap to kill the aphid colony before it spreads back to the tomatoes.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Borage flowers attract bumblebees and honey bees that improve tomato fruit set through buzz pollination. Reputed to deter tomato hornworm; folklore claims flavor improvement.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Carrots fit under sprawling tomato canopy; different root depth (carrots deep, tomato mid-deep) minimizes competition. The pairing is the title of Riotte's classic gardening canon.
entries.source: Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Tomato canopy shades carrot tops during late summer heat, slowing bolting and reducing greening on exposed shoulders of carrot roots.
entries.source: Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Parsley flowers (in its second biennial year) attract parasitic wasps and hoverflies that prey on tomato hornworm and aphids.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Asparagus produces compounds (asparagine derivatives) that deter root-knot nematodes around tomato roots. The reciprocal: tomato deters asparagus beetle. Long-standing mutual pairing in extension literature.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Tomato leaves contain solanine that deters asparagus beetle; planting tomatoes adjacent to permanent asparagus beds protects spear development from feeding damage.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Dill flowers (umbels) attract parasitic wasps (Trichogramma, Cotesia) and hoverflies that prey on cabbage worm and cabbage looper larvae. Let some dill bolt for sustained flowering.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as with cabbage — dill's umbel flowers draw beneficial parasitoid wasps and hoverflies, controlling cabbage moth larvae on broccoli.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Dill flowers draw parasitoid wasps that target cabbage moth caterpillars and aphids on cauliflower curds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Long-season brussels sprouts benefit from sustained beneficial-insect populations supported by bolted dill flowers through summer.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bolted dill flowers attract hoverflies and parasitic wasps that control aphid colonies on kale.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Thyme's volatile oils (thymol, carvacrol) deter cabbage moth (Pieris rapae). Most effective when planted as a perimeter border around brassica beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Sage repels cabbage moth and cabbage looper; the traditional pairing of European kitchen gardens. Aromatic perimeter planting works best.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Rosemary deters cabbage moth via aromatic oils; effective as a permanent perimeter shrub around brassica beds in mediterranean and warm continental climates.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Oregano's volatile oils deter cabbage moth and aphids; useful aromatic perimeter planting around brassica beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts cabbage white butterflies (Pieris rapae) as an egg-laying decoy, drawing pressure away from the cabbage heads. Inspect nasturtium weekly for eggs and caterpillars.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage — nasturtium decoys cabbage white butterflies away from broccoli heads.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic interplanted with roses reduces aphid and Japanese beetle pressure; sulfur compounds in garlic act as a fungistat against black spot. The classic rose-garden companion pairing.
entries.source: UC ANR; American Rose Society

entries.description: Garlic planted around fruit trees deters borers and aphids; traditional orchard guild planting. Sulfur compounds and aromatic volatiles disrupt pest host-finding.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; traditional orchard guides

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts aphids and cucumber beetles away from cucumber vines as a sacrificial host. Best planted at the ends of cucumber rows.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Dill flowers bring beneficial insects (hoverflies, parasitic wasps) that control cucumber pests; minor traditional claim of pickling-flavor synergy when grown adjacent.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Radishes planted among cucumber vines attract cucumber beetles and flea beetles; let some bolt to flower and continue trapping. Sacrificial radishes can be removed and replanted as needed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion's volatile sulfur compounds confuse and deter carrot rust fly (Psila rosae), one of the worst carrot pests in temperate gardens. Interplant in alternating rows. The classic mutual European-tradition pairing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Carrot foliage scent reciprocally deters onion fly and thrips; less strongly supported than the reverse direction but still a documented benefit.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion beds adjacent to tomato deter aphids and some leaf miners via sulfur compound volatiles.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Leeks provide the same carrot-rust-fly deterrent as onion via shared allium chemistry; useful where onion bulbing is unreliable.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Chives deter carrot rust fly through allium volatiles, with the bonus of an edible perennial border crop.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chive border around roses deters aphids and reduces black spot through sulfur compounds in the plant tissue. Smaller-scale alternative to garlic interplanting.
entries.source: American Rose Society

entries.description: Garlic chive (Allium tuberosum) provides milder allium deterrence than bulb garlic; less root competition with tomato. Perennial border option.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Radishes mature in 25-30 days and are harvested before lettuce reaches full size — often interplanted in the same row to maximize bed productivity.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as leaf lettuce — radishes mature quickly and clear the row before head lettuce needs the full space.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Radish attracts flea beetles away from young bean plants; allow some radishes to flower to maintain trap activity through bean establishment.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Cucumber vines crawl through corn rows, shading soil and suppressing weeds at the bed level. Don't combine with squash in the same row — only one ground-layer crop per bed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Yarrow's flat umbel flowers attract hoverflies (Syrphidae) and parasitic wasps that prey on tomato hornworm and aphids. Drought-tolerant perennial — plant in bed corners.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Yarrow draws hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that control cabbage moth larvae and aphids.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Calendula attracts beneficial insects (hoverflies, lacewings) and reportedly deters cabbage white butterfly through scent. Edible flowers for garnish.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Calendula's bright orange composite flowers attract hoverflies and bees; minor pest-deterrent claims for whitefly.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Borage greatly improves bee visitation to strawberry flowers; documented yield increase in trial plots, especially in cool climates where strawberries flower before native pollinator populations build.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide; UK Bumblebee Conservation Trust

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts squash bugs and cucumber beetles away from squash vines; plant at the ends of squash rows for trap interception.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as winter squash — nasturtium decoys squash bugs and cucumber beetles.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Spearmint deters cabbage moth, cabbage looper, and ants. Plant in containers sunk in the bed to limit mint's invasive runner spread.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Mint deters whitefly and aphids near tomatoes. Always grow in containers around the tomato bed — never plant runners directly.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Lavender deters cabbage moth and many other lepidopteran pests through camphor-rich volatiles. Aromatic perimeter planting in dry mediterranean and continental climates.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Fennel produces anethole and other allelopathic compounds that inhibit germination and growth of nearby crops; tomato is severely affected. Plant fennel in its own bed at the property edge.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Florence fennel exhibits the same allelopathic effects as herb fennel — anethole and related volatiles inhibit tomato growth.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fennel inhibits bean growth via allelopathic root and leaf compounds; observed stunting at planting distances under 4 ft.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as bush bean — pole bean is also severely affected by fennel's allelopathic chemistry.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Snap pea is allelopathically inhibited by adjacent fennel; legume growth and pod set both reduced.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fennel and dill cross-pollinate at the seed stage if both are allowed to flower; also exhibit mutual allelopathic effects when planted adjacent. Save seed from only one of the two each season.
entries.source: Seed Savers Exchange; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Fennel inhibits cilantro germination and growth via shared apiaceae-family allelopathic compounds; keep separated in the garden plan.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fennel allelochemistry stunts brassica seedling establishment; observed reduction in head formation when planted under 4 ft separation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage — fennel stunts broccoli head development at close range.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic's sulfur compounds inhibit Rhizobium nitrogen-fixing bacteria in bean root nodules, reducing nitrogen fixation and stunting growth. Plant at minimum 3 ft separation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as bush bean — garlic disrupts Rhizobium symbiosis in pole bean nodules.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic inhibits pea nodule Rhizobium activity; pea growth and pod set reduced when planted under 3 ft separation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as snap pea — garlic disrupts nodule formation in all leguminous pea species.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic strongly inhibits fava bean nitrogen fixation; keep at minimum 3 ft separation from any allium bed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion sulfur compounds disrupt Rhizobium symbiosis in bean nodules, reducing nitrogen fixation and stunting growth.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as bush bean — onion disrupts pole bean Rhizobium activity.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion volatiles inhibit pea nodule formation and pod set; keep separated by 3 ft minimum.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Leek shares allium-family allelopathic effects on bean Rhizobium symbiosis, though less pronounced than bulb garlic or onion.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Chive sulfur compounds inhibit bean nodule activity; effect mild but documented. Keep chive perennial bed away from bean rotation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Brassicas share Verticillium wilt with strawberry; same-bed planting accumulates pathogens. Also compete for soil calcium. Rotate 3+ years between brassica and strawberry beds.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage — broccoli and strawberry share Verticillium wilt pathogen.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Kale shares Verticillium wilt with strawberry; rotate 3+ years before planting kale where strawberries grew (and vice versa).
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Cauliflower and strawberry share Verticillium wilt; observe 3-year rotation between them in the same bed.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Potato and tomato share late blight (Phytophthora infestans), early blight (Alternaria solani), and Verticillium wilt. Rotate 3-4 years between nightshade beds; never plant adjacent.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same disease sharing as potato → tomato. Either direction concentrates blight, early blight, and Verticillium pathogens in the soil.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Eggplant and tomato share Verticillium and Fusarium wilts; same-bed planting accumulates pathogens. Rotate beds across the nightshade family.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Eggplant and potato share Colorado potato beetle and Verticillium wilt pathogen; rotate as part of nightshade-family planning.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Dill cross-pollinates with carrot when both are flowering, degrading carrot seed quality for replanting. Dill flowers also stunt carrot root growth in close proximity at flowering. Beneficial earlier in the season; problematic when seed-saving.
entries.source: Seed Savers Exchange

entries.description: Wormwood (Artemisia absinthium) produces absinthin and santonin — powerful allelopathic compounds. Keep wormwood well away from vegetable beds; minimum 6 ft separation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato — wormwood absinthin severely inhibits bean germination and growth.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Kohlrabi and tomato compete heavily for soil potassium and calcium; observed yield reduction on both crops when planted in the same bed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic inhibits asparagus growth via shared sulfur compounds and root competition. The opposite pattern from tomato — never plant alliums in or near asparagus beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as garlic — onion sulfur compounds inhibit asparagus development.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bush beans fix nitrogen via Rhizobium nodules, available to adjacent corn over the season. Non-climbing habit avoids competing with corn stalks for canopy space.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research; Iroquois agricultural tradition

entries.description: Bush beans planted in rotation or as adjacent interplant supply nitrogen to heavy-feeding brassicas; rotation works better than same-bed in most climates.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fava beans fix nitrogen efficiently in cool conditions and tolerate brassica-friendly temperatures; plant before or alongside spring brassicas to boost the crop.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage — fava bean nitrogen fixation is a strong fit for spring broccoli's heavy nitrogen demand.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Crimson clover winter cover crop tilled or rolled in spring releases substantial nitrogen for the tomato crop; reduces synthetic fertilizer needs by 30-50% in trials.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension cover crop guides; SARE

entries.description: Crimson clover preceding heavy-feeding sweet corn supplies 70-130 lb N/acre when terminated at full bloom. Standard rotation in organic systems.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: White clover living mulch under fruit trees fixes nitrogen and attracts beneficial insects; reduces mowing frequency and competes well with weeds.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; orchard guild literature

entries.description: Same mechanism as apple — white clover living-mulch ring around pear trees fixes nitrogen and supports beneficials.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; orchard guild literature

entries.description: Hairy vetch winter cover crop fixes 100-150 lb N/acre; rolled or tilled before tomato transplant, no-till tomato production with vetch residue mulch is a documented yield-boost technique.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; USDA-ARS Beltsville no-till research

entries.description: Winter pea cover crop fixes nitrogen for spring brassica beds; especially effective in mild winters where vetch may not establish.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Red clover preceding or interseeded with corn fixes substantial nitrogen; longer-lived than crimson clover, fits multi-year rotation.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Rosemary deters carrot rust fly via aromatic oils; permanent rosemary shrub at the corner of carrot beds gives season-long protection in mild climates.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Sage repels carrot rust fly with similar mechanism to rosemary; aromatic perimeter planting.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Mustard cover crop attracts harlequin bug and flea beetle away from the main brassica crop; mow or remove the mustard before pests disperse back. Also biofumigates the soil when tilled in.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; UC IPM

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts aphids and Mexican bean beetle away from bean plants; sacrificial host.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: White clover living mulch suppresses weeds between corn rows and fixes some nitrogen. Mow before corn pollinates to avoid competition.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Lacy phacelia is among the most attractive bee plants known; massively increases honey-bee and bumblebee visitation to cucurbit flowers. Documented yield boost in pollinator-limited regions.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber — phacelia draws bees to squash flowers, increasing female-flower fruit set rates.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Tomato is self-pollinating but bumblebee buzz-pollination significantly improves fruit set, especially in cool maritime climates. Phacelia draws those bumblebees.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Sunflower's large composite flowers attract diverse native and managed pollinators; cucumber yield benefits from increased bee visitation.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cilantro (when bolted) attracts hoverflies and parasitic wasps that prey on aphids. Let some cilantro flower for the beneficials, save the seed as coriander.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Parsley flowers attract beneficial insects (parasitic wasps, hoverflies) in permanent asparagus beds; supports asparagus-beetle predator populations.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Anise hyssop (Agastache foeniculum) is a top-tier bee magnet; improves squash pollination and supports beneficial-insect habitat.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cilantro flowers attract hoverflies that prey on bean aphids; bolt cilantro intentionally near beans for sustained beneficial-insect support.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Native bee balm (Monarda fistulosa, M. didyma) attracts bumblebees whose buzz pollination boosts tomato fruit set; also attracts hummingbirds and beneficial wasps. Long bloom season.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Pepper benefits from increased bee visitation that bee balm draws; native plant supports broader pollinator-population health.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as pepper — eggplant flowers benefit from bumblebee buzz pollination, which bee balm enhances.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Native purple coneflower attracts native bees, butterflies, and parasitic wasps; long bloom period (8+ weeks) supports pollinators throughout cucurbit flowering.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber — echinacea supports pollinators near squash beds through a long blooming window.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Native common milkweed (Asclepias syriaca) supports monarch butterflies, native bees, and beneficial wasps near squash. Plant in bed corners; sap is toxic so site away from where children harvest.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Classic fruit-tree guild plant — comfrey's deep taproot mines subsoil potassium and phosphorus; foliage chopped and dropped serves as a nutrient-rich mulch. Suppresses weeds under tree canopy.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; UK orchard guild literature

entries.description: Same mechanism as apple — comfrey ring under pear tree provides chop-and-drop fertility and weed suppression.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; UK orchard guild literature

entries.description: Comfrey under peach trees mines potassium for stone fruit's heavy K demand; mulch with cut foliage 3-4 times per season.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute

entries.description: Same mechanism as peach and apple, comfrey under plum trees provides chop-drop fertility cycling.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute

entries.description: Thyme's thymol and carvacrol volatiles deter cabbage moth (Pieris rapae) and cabbage looper. Plant as a perimeter border to broccoli beds for season-long pest pressure reduction.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Thyme volatiles repel cabbage moth and aphids on kale. Aromatic perennial border works season-long; trim to keep flowering for beneficial insects.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Long-season brussels sprouts benefit from a permanent thyme perimeter; sustained aromatic deterrence of cabbage moth and cabbage looper through fall harvest.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Thyme volatiles deter cabbage moth from forming larvae in cauliflower curds. Border planting more effective than scattered interplanting.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Thyme deters cabbage moth and looper on collards; particularly useful in southern gardens where brassica pest pressure runs longer.
entries.source: UF/IFAS Extension; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Sage's camphor-rich volatile oils repel cabbage moth and cabbage looper on broccoli. Aromatic perimeter planting is the traditional European kitchen-garden arrangement.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as broccoli, sage volatiles deter cabbage moth from depositing eggs near cauliflower heads.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Sage deters cabbage moth and aphids on kale; the perennial habit makes sage a low-maintenance permanent companion at the bed edge.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Long brussels sprouts season benefits from sage's season-long aromatic deterrence of cabbage moth and looper.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Rosemary deters cabbage moth via camphor-rich volatiles. Best as a permanent perimeter shrub in mediterranean and warm continental climates where rosemary overwinters.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Rosemary volatiles deter cabbage moth on kale; pair the two as a permanent kitchen-garden block where winter survival allows.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Rosemary as a perimeter shrub protects long-season brussels sprouts from cabbage moth and looper egg-laying.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Rosemary's aromatic oils deter cabbage moth and looper from cauliflower heads.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Oregano's carvacrol and thymol volatiles deter cabbage moth and aphids. Aromatic perimeter planting around cabbage beds works best.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Oregano volatiles repel cabbage moth and aphids on kale; perennial border that doubles as a culinary harvest.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, oregano deters cabbage moth and aphids from forming on cauliflower curds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Oregano border deters cabbage moth and looper through the long brussels sprouts season.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Spearmint deters cabbage moth, cabbage looper, and flea beetle on broccoli. Always grow in sunken containers to limit invasive runner spread.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Spearmint deters cabbage moth and flea beetle on kale; container planting at the bed edge avoids runner takeover.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Spearmint repels cabbage moth and looper through the long brussels sprouts season. Sink containers in the bed border, never direct-plant runners.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Lavender deters cabbage moth and many lepidopteran pests through camphor-rich volatiles. Most effective as a permanent perimeter shrub in dry climates.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Lavender volatiles repel cabbage moth on kale; bonus pollinator support from long bloom window.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Lavender deters cabbage moth from depositing eggs on cauliflower curds; mediterranean climate pairing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Hyssop (Hyssopus officinalis) deters cabbage moth via pinocamphone-rich oil; traditional monastery-garden border around brassicas.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, hyssop oils repel cabbage moth from broccoli; also attracts honeybees and beneficial wasps when flowering.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Tansy (Tanacetum vulgare) produces thujone and camphor volatiles that deter cabbage moth and flea beetle. Caution: tansy is toxic to livestock and self-seeds aggressively, keep contained.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Tansy oils deter cabbage moth and aphids on broccoli; bonus repellent effect on ants and Japanese beetle.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Feverfew (Tanacetum parthenium) deters aphids and cabbage moth via parthenolide and pyrethrum-like compounds. Long bloom season also attracts hoverflies.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, feverfew deters aphids and cabbage moth from broccoli through pyrethrin-related compounds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Nasturtium decoys cabbage white butterflies and aphids away from kale; sacrificial host that gardeners can spray with insecticidal soap when colonies build.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts cabbage moth and aphid pressure away from long-season brussels sprouts. Inspect the trap weekly through fall.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Nasturtium decoys cabbage white butterflies away from cauliflower heads; place at row ends and remove infested foliage before pests disperse back.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, nasturtium pulls cabbage moth and aphid pressure away from collard greens; useful in southern gardens with extended pest seasons.
entries.source: UF/IFAS Extension

entries.description: Yarrow's flat umbel flowers attract hoverflies and parasitic wasps (Trichogramma, Cotesia) that prey on cabbage moth larvae and aphids. Drought-tolerant perennial.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Yarrow draws hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that control aphid colonies on kale; sustained bloom through summer keeps beneficial populations active.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Long brussels sprouts season benefits from yarrow's drought-tolerant, sustained beneficial-insect support.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Yarrow attracts hoverflies and parasitic wasps that control aphids on cauliflower curds.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Calendula attracts hoverflies, lacewings, and parasitoid wasps that prey on cabbage moth larvae and aphids. Reportedly repels cabbage white butterfly by scent.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, calendula draws beneficials that target aphids on kale. Edible flowers double as garnish.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Calendula attracts hoverflies and lacewings that prey on aphids before they reach developing cauliflower curds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Calendula's long bloom window supports beneficial insects through the brussels sprouts long growing season.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Sweet alyssum is among the most documented hoverfly-attracting plants. Hoverfly larvae are voracious aphid predators; trial work shows reduced brassica aphid pressure with alyssum interplanting.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as broccoli, alyssum draws hoverflies that target cabbage aphid colonies. Plant along bed edges for continuous bloom.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Sweet alyssum hoverfly attraction reduces aphid pressure on kale; low-growing habit fits the front of any brassica bed.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Alyssum hoverflies protect developing cauliflower curds from aphid colonies that disfigure heads at harvest.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Bolted cilantro flowers attract hoverflies and parasitic wasps that prey on cabbage moth larvae and aphids. Let some plants flower intentionally before pulling.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as broccoli, cilantro umbels draw beneficial insects that target cabbage aphid and cabbage moth larvae.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bolted cilantro near kale supports hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that suppress aphid colonies.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bush beans fix nitrogen via Rhizobium nodules and supply heavy-feeding broccoli. Rotation works better than same-bed; avoid alliums in the rotation block.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bush beans in rotation or as adjacent interplant supply nitrogen to kale; useful in the same-year rotation slot or as a preceding crop.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as broccoli, bush bean nitrogen supports cauliflower's heavy nitrogen demand for head formation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fava beans fix nitrogen efficiently in cool conditions and pair with cool-season kale beds. Sow favas before or alongside spring kale planting.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Fava bean nitrogen fixation precedes the long brussels sprouts season; terminate favas in late spring and follow with sprouts transplants for residual N.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Crimson clover winter cover crop terminated in spring releases 70-130 lb N/acre for heavy-feeding cabbage; reduces synthetic fertilizer needs substantially.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, crimson clover precedes spring broccoli to supply nitrogen for head development.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Winter pea cover crop fixes nitrogen for spring broccoli beds; particularly effective in mild winters where hairy vetch may not establish.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Onion sulfur compounds deter leaf miner and flea beetle on beet foliage. Loose-soil preference of onion bulbs matches well with beet's own root habit.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Chives deter leaf miner and flea beetle on beets; perennial border that doubles as an edible harvest.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Bush beans fix nitrogen for adjacent beets in rotation; avoid alliums in the same bed since beets and alliums can compete for surface root zone.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Lettuce occupies the upper canopy while beets develop roots below; harvest the lettuce as beets size up. Classic intercropping pair.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Kohlrabi globes sit above-ground while beets fill the soil below; matched harvest window in cool weather.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Radish seeded with carrot germinates fast and marks the carrot rows; quick radish harvest loosens soil for slower carrots and reduces thinning needs.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Radish matures in 25-30 days and is harvested before spinach reaches full size; share the same cool-season row efficiently.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Radish clears the row before chard fills out; uses spring soil moisture efficiently while chard establishes.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Both fast-growing cool-season crops; alternating radish and arugula in the same row maximizes early-season bed productivity.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Carrots develop below-ground while leaf lettuce occupies the canopy. Lettuce harvest finishes before carrots need full space.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Spinach occupies the upper canopy through cool weather while carrots develop roots; spinach harvest finishes before carrots size up.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Spearmint volatiles deter carrot rust fly; container-grow mint at the bed corner to avoid runner invasion.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Shallot shares allium-family sulfur compounds with onion and leek; deters carrot rust fly via the same chemistry. Smaller bulbs fit tighter spacing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion border around strawberry beds deters aphids and reduces slug pressure through sulfur compound volatiles.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Garlic interplanted at strawberry bed edges deters aphids, mites, and reduces fungal disease pressure through sulfur volatiles. Plant garlic cloves in fall around the perennial bed.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Onion border deters cabbage moth and aphids on cabbage via sulfur compound volatiles. Keep at least 1 ft separation from the cabbage itself to avoid root competition.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, onion sulfur compounds repel cabbage moth and aphids near broccoli.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion volatiles deter cabbage moth and aphids on kale; useful interplant or perimeter row.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion border deters aphids, thrips, and some leaf miners on sweet pepper through sulfur volatiles.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as sweet pepper, onion volatiles repel flea beetle and aphids near eggplant.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic interplanted with tomato deters aphids, spider mites, and some leaf miners through sulfur compound volatiles. Plant cloves at the corners of tomato cages in fall for spring harvest before tomatoes fill out.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic deters aphids, thrips, and spider mites near pepper plants; sulfur volatiles disrupt pest host-finding.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic interplant deters flea beetle, aphids, and Colorado potato beetle near eggplant.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic sulfur volatiles deter cabbage moth and aphids on cabbage; the brassica-allium pairing is a long-standing kitchen-garden traditional.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, garlic interplant deters cabbage moth and aphids around broccoli.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Garlic planted around peach trees deters borers and aphids; traditional orchard guild planting that disrupts pest host-finding through sulfur volatiles.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute; traditional orchard guides

entries.description: Garlic deters cane borers and aphids near raspberry; interplant cloves at the base of cane rows in fall.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Garlic at the dripline of pear trees deters borers, aphids, and pear psylla through sulfur volatiles; classic permaculture guild plant.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute

entries.description: Leek allium volatiles deter aphids and thrips near tomato, with less bulb root competition than full-size onion.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Leek interplant deters cabbage moth and aphids on cabbage; allium chemistry shared with onion.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, leek allium volatiles deter cabbage moth and aphids near broccoli.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Shallot deters aphids, leaf miners, and thrips near tomato through sulfur compound volatiles; smaller bulb fits tight spacing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chive border deters aphids and spider mites near tomato; perennial allium that flowers early for pollinators.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chive ring under apple trees deters aphids, ants, and reduces apple scab incidence via sulfur compounds. Lower-maintenance than bulb garlic.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute

entries.description: Chive border around lettuce deters aphids; flowering chive also attracts hoverflies that prey on aphid colonies.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chive border around strawberry beds deters aphids and reduces fungal disease through sulfur volatiles; perennial habit matches strawberry's permanent bed.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Onion volatiles inhibit pea nodule formation in snow pea; keep separated by 3 ft minimum. Same mechanism as snap pea.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Onion sulfur compounds disrupt Rhizobium nitrogen fixation in fava bean root nodules. Keep all alliums at minimum 3 ft from fava beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Chive sulfur compounds inhibit pole bean nodule activity; effect mild but documented. Keep chive bed away from bean rotation.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chive inhibits Rhizobium nodulation in snap pea; same allium chemistry as garlic and onion, mild but measurable.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts squash bugs and cucumber beetles away from pumpkin vines as a sacrificial host. Plant at row ends or vine perimeter.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Same mechanism as squash, nasturtium decoys cucumber beetle (vector of bacterial wilt) and squash bug away from cantaloupe.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Nasturtium pulls aphids and cucumber beetles away from watermelon; plant at the ends of long sprawling rows.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Nasturtium decoys squash bugs and cucumber beetles away from zucchini plants; sacrificial host that can be sprayed with insecticidal soap when colonies build.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Borage flowers attract bumblebees and honey bees that improve cucumber fruit set, particularly in cool maritime climates where pollinator activity is limited.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Borage massively increases bee visitation to squash flowers, improving female-flower fruit set rates. Reputed to deter squash bug; folklore-only on that point.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as winter squash, borage draws bees to summer squash flowers and supports continuous fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Borage draws bumblebees and honeybees to cantaloupe flowers; improves fruit set in cool short-season climates.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Borage attracts bumblebees whose buzz pollination significantly improves pepper fruit set, especially in cool climates.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Eggplant flowers benefit from bumblebee buzz pollination; borage is among the most reliable bumblebee draws.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Dill umbel flowers attract parasitic wasps and hoverflies that prey on squash bug nymphs and aphids; let dill bolt for sustained beneficial-insect support.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as winter squash, dill flowers support beneficials that target squash bug nymphs on summer squash.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Radish attracts squash vine borer and cucumber beetle away from squash plants; let some radishes flower to sustain trap activity through squash vining.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Radish acts as a sacrificial host for cucumber beetle and squash bug near summer squash; remove infested plants before pests disperse back.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Radish at row ends pulls cucumber beetle and squash bug pressure away from pumpkin vines through the vining season.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: French marigold (Tagetes patula) suppresses root-knot nematodes near cucumber roots via alpha-terthienyl. Plant 12-18 inches from cucumber base.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber, marigold root chemistry suppresses root-knot nematodes around winter squash. Bonus aphid and whitefly deterrence above ground.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Marigold nematode suppression around cantaloupe vines reduces yield losses on infested ground; particularly valuable in repeatedly-cropped melon beds.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Marigold suppresses root-knot nematodes around watermelon roots; standard southern-garden interplant where nematodes limit melon yields.
entries.source: UC ANR; UF/IFAS Extension; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Lacy phacelia is among the top bee-attracting plants known; massively increases honey-bee and bumblebee visitation to cantaloupe flowers and improves fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as winter squash, phacelia draws bees to summer squash flowers and improves female-flower fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Phacelia improves pollination of pumpkin's large female flowers; long-bloom habit covers the full pumpkin vining season.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Sunflower's large composite flowers attract diverse native and managed pollinators; winter squash benefits from increased bee visitation to female flowers.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Sunflower draws bees and native pollinators that improve cantaloupe fruit set; can also serve as a windbreak for sprawling melon rows.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Native bee balm (Monarda) attracts bumblebees and hummingbirds; cucumber benefits from sustained native bee visitation to flowers.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber, bee balm draws native bees that improve squash flower fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Anise hyssop is a top-tier bee magnet whose long bloom period (8-10 weeks) overlaps cucumber flowering; documented yield boost in pollinator-limited regions.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber, anise hyssop sustains pollinator populations through the long summer squash season.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Chive border deters aphids on head lettuce; flowering chives also attract hoverflies that prey on aphid colonies.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Chervil deters slugs and aphids near lettuce through anethole-related volatiles; cool-season annual that grows in lettuce's preferred conditions.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Nasturtium decoys aphids and leaf miners away from chard leaves; remove infested foliage before colonies spread back to the chard.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: Bolted dill flowers attract hoverflies and parasitic wasps that suppress aphid colonies on lettuce.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Dill flowers draw hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that prey on onion thrips and aphids in storage onion beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Bolted cilantro near spinach attracts hoverflies that prey on aphids and leaf miners; both crops share cool-season timing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Basil volatiles repel aphids, whiteflies, and thrips on sweet pepper; same protective effect as the classic basil-tomato pairing.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as sweet pepper, basil aromatic oils deter aphids and thrips on hot pepper.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Basil deters flea beetle, aphids, and whitefly near eggplant; aromatic oils disrupt pest host-finding behavior.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Basil interplanted at asparagus bed edges deters asparagus beetle and aphids through volatile oils.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Parsley flowers (in its second biennial year) attract parasitic wasps and hoverflies that prey on aphids and thrips near pepper.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Bolted parsley umbel flowers draw beneficial insects that target carrot pests; bonus, both are apiaceae and can share rotation block.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Bolted cilantro attracts hoverflies that prey on pepper aphids; let some plants flower intentionally for sustained beneficial-insect support.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Yarrow's umbel flowers draw hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that target carrot rust fly and aphids. Drought-tolerant perennial fits the bed corner.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Yarrow attracts hoverflies and parasitic wasps that prey on pepper aphids and thrips.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as pepper, yarrow draws beneficials that target aphids and flea beetle near eggplant.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Yarrow attracts hoverflies that prey on strawberry aphids and tarnished plant bug; perennial habit fits the permanent strawberry bed.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Zinnia attracts butterflies, native bees, and hummingbirds; bumblebees drawn by zinnia improve tomato buzz pollination and fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Zinnia draws bumblebees and native bees that improve pepper flower fruit set; long bloom window covers the full pepper season.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Zinnia attracts bees and butterflies that improve cucumber pollination; place at row ends for visibility to passing pollinators.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Zinnia draws bumblebees that improve squash female-flower fruit set; bonus visual screen for sprawling squash beds.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Zinnia attracts bumblebees whose buzz pollination significantly improves eggplant fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cosmos attracts hoverflies, lacewings, and parasitic wasps that prey on tomato hornworm and aphids; long bloom window through tomato fruit set.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cosmos draws hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that target pepper aphids and thrips; minor pollinator boost from bee visitation.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cosmos attracts beneficial insects that prey on cabbage moth larvae and aphids on broccoli.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Cosmos draws bees that improve cucumber pollination; place at sunny bed corners for full visibility to pollinators.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Sweet alyssum draws hoverflies whose larvae are top aphid predators near tomato; UC ANR trials show measurable aphid suppression with alyssum interplanting.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato, alyssum hoverflies reduce aphid colonies on pepper plants.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Alyssum hoverflies prey on aphids near cucumber; also draws minor bee visitation that improves pollination.
entries.source: UC ANR; Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Tansy at the base of raspberry canes deters cane borer and Japanese beetle through thujone-camphor volatiles. Caution: tansy is toxic to livestock and self-seeds aggressively.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Tansy at the dripline deters codling moth and Japanese beetle near apple trees; classic orchard guild plant.
entries.source: Permaculture Research Institute

entries.description: Tansy border around strawberry beds deters aphids, ants, and Japanese beetle. Keep contained to prevent self-seeding into the perennial bed.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Feverfew deters aphids near rose plantings via parthenolide and pyrethrum-related compounds; long-blooming companion that attracts hoverflies as a bonus.
entries.source: American Rose Society

entries.description: French marigold (Tagetes patula) suppresses root-knot nematodes near pepper roots via alpha-terthienyl; same nematode chemistry as the marigold-tomato pairing.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato and pepper, marigold root chemistry suppresses root-knot nematodes around eggplant. Bonus aphid and whitefly deterrence above ground.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Marigold alpha-terthienyl suppresses root-knot and root-lesion nematodes around potato; particularly valuable in repeatedly-cropped potato beds.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Marigold deters Mexican bean beetle and aphids near bush bean; nematode suppression as a secondary benefit for any rotation crop.
entries.source: UC ANR; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Buckwheat blooms within 30 days of seeding and is among the strongest hoverfly and honey bee draws available; massively increases cucumber flower visitation. Often grown as a summer cover-strip alongside cucurbits.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Xerces Society

entries.description: Same mechanism as cucumber, buckwheat draws bees that improve squash female-flower fruit set; quick-bloom annual fits the squash vining season.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Xerces Society

entries.description: Buckwheat preceding spring broccoli (as a late-summer cover the year before) supports beneficial-insect populations and suppresses weeds; flowers attract hoverflies that prey on broccoli aphids.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Xerces Society

entries.description: Winter rye cover crop terminated and rolled in spring forms a thick mulch; no-till tomato transplants into the rye residue see improved moisture retention and reduced early blight splashback. Standard in organic systems.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; USDA-ARS Beltsville no-till research

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato, rolled rye residue mulch suppresses weeds and reduces splash-borne disease near pepper transplants.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Winter rye terminated in spring forms a weed-suppressing mulch for squash transplants; rye allelopathy reduces small-seeded weed germination during establishment.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Winter rye produces benzoxazinoid (DIBOA, BOA) allelochemicals that suppress germination of small-seeded crops including lettuce; wait 3-4 weeks after rye termination before direct-seeding lettuce.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; USDA-ARS allelopathy research

entries.description: Oats winter-kill in zones 6 and colder, leaving a weed-suppressing residue mat for spring cabbage transplants. No tillage needed before planting.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Same mechanism as cabbage, winter-killed oats form a clean mulch for spring broccoli transplants.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Daikon cover (tillage radish) bio-drills compacted subsoil; deep taproots winter-kill in zones 7 and colder, leaving open channels and decomposing organic matter that improves drainage for spring tomatoes.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato, daikon cover crop's deep bio-drilling improves drainage and root penetration for spring pepper transplants.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Daikon cover is a brassica and shares clubroot (Plasmodiophora brassicae) with cabbage; rotate 3+ years between brassica cover crops and brassica vegetable crops on infected ground.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Hairy vetch winter cover fixes 100-150 lb N/acre for heavy-feeding sweet corn; rolled or tilled before planting. One of the most cost-effective N sources in organic systems.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; USDA-ARS Beltsville

entries.description: Hairy vetch terminated in spring releases substantial nitrogen for heavy-feeding broccoli; standard organic rotation before brassica beds.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Red clover preceding cabbage fixes nitrogen for heavy-feeding brassicas; longer-lived than crimson clover and fits multi-year rotations.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Black walnut (Juglans nigra) roots, leaves, and nut hulls release juglone (5-hydroxy-1,4-naphthoquinone), which is highly toxic to tomato. Symptoms include wilting and yellowing within the dripline plus 20-30 ft beyond. Never plant tomato within 50-80 ft of a mature black walnut.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension; OSU Extension

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato, sweet pepper is severely sensitive to black walnut juglone. Keep peppers at least 50-80 ft from any mature black walnut tree.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Hot pepper shares the same juglone sensitivity as sweet pepper; avoid planting within the black walnut root zone.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Eggplant is highly sensitive to juglone, showing wilting and plant death within the black walnut root zone. Plant eggplant in raised beds with imported soil if proximity is unavoidable.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Potato shows reduced tuber yield and foliar wilting under juglone exposure; never site potato beds within the black walnut dripline plus a 30 ft buffer.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Blueberry is among the most juglone-sensitive crops; plants decline within the root zone. Site blueberry beds well clear of any black walnut, or use isolated containers.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: Apple trees show stunting and reduced yield within the black walnut root zone via juglone exposure. Most cultivars are moderately to highly sensitive; plant orchards well away from existing black walnut.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; OSU Extension

entries.description: Blackberry is sensitive to juglone and shows reduced cane vigor in the black walnut root zone; site cane rows clear of the dripline plus a generous buffer.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research; Penn State Extension

entries.description: English walnut (Juglans regia) produces less juglone than black walnut but still affects sensitive crops; keep tomatoes at least 30 ft from mature trees.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research

entries.description: Blueberry shows decline near English walnut at closer range than less-sensitive crops; avoid siting blueberries inside the dripline.
entries.source: USDA Forest Service juglone toxicity research

entries.description: Sunflower roots and decomposing residues release allelochemicals (chlorogenic and isochlorogenic acids) that suppress potato emergence and tuber growth; keep at least 4 ft separation and avoid sunflower residue in potato beds.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy research; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Sunflower allelochemicals suppress bean germination and early growth; observed stunting at planting distances under 3 ft.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy research

entries.description: Same mechanism as bush bean, sunflower hulls and root exudates inhibit pole bean establishment when planted in close proximity.
entries.source: USDA-ARS allelopathy research

entries.description: Bush beans in the rotation before strawberry plantings supply nitrogen for fruit development; avoid same-bed alliums in either crop's slot.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Spinach occupies the gaps between strawberry rows in spring and is harvested before strawberries fill the bed; uses cool early-season conditions efficiently.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide

entries.description: Thyme deters slugs and reduces aphid pressure near strawberry; perennial ground-hugging habit fits the permanent bed without root competition.
entries.source: UC ANR strawberry production guide; Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Tall corn provides afternoon shade to bush beans, reducing heat stress and improving pod set in arid and continental summers.
entries.source: USDA-ARS Three Sisters research

entries.description: Calendula attracts hoverflies and lacewings whose larvae prey on aphids and carrot rust fly eggs.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Xerces Society

entries.description: Bolted cilantro attracts hoverflies and parasitic wasps that prey on squash bug nymphs and aphids.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Basil's volatile oils deter carrot fly and aphids; useful interplant in late summer when basil is at peak aromatic strength.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Lemon balm citral and citronellal volatiles deter aphids and whiteflies near tomato; bonus, flowering lemon balm attracts honeybees.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Marjoram volatiles deter cabbage moth; similar oregano-family chemistry but milder. Aromatic perimeter planting around cabbage beds.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Summer savory volatiles deter Mexican bean beetle and aphids; the classic European companion to bush beans, often planted in alternating rows.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Same mechanism as bush bean, summer savory deters Mexican bean beetle near pole beans.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: Horseradish planted at the corners of potato beds deters Colorado potato beetle through glucosinolate-mustard-oil volatiles; perennial habit, plant in containers to limit invasive root spread.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Cunningham, Great Garden Companions

entries.description: French tarragon's anise-like volatiles deter aphids and whiteflies near tomato; perennial in zones 5-9.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension

entries.description: Nasturtium attracts flea beetles, aphids, and whiteflies away from eggplant; sacrificial host that can be sprayed with insecticidal soap or removed when colonies build.
entries.source: Cornell Cooperative Extension; Riotte, Carrots Love Tomatoes

entries.description: African marigold (Tagetes erecta) produces alpha-terthienyl-class thiophenes effective against root-knot nematodes around tomato; taller habit than French marigold.
entries.source: UC ANR; USDA-ARS nematology research

entries.description: Lacy phacelia (Phacelia tanacetifolia, distinct slug from generic phacelia) is among the top bumblebee plants known; bumblebee buzz pollination improves tomato fruit set in cool maritime climates.
entries.source: Xerces Society pollinator habitat publications

entries.description: Field pea cover crop terminated in spring supplies nitrogen for heavy-feeding sweet corn; tolerates colder soil at sowing than hairy vetch.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Common vetch (Vicia sativa) winter cover fixes nitrogen for spring corn; less cold-hardy than hairy vetch but easier to terminate.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Berseem clover (Trifolium alexandrinum) fixes nitrogen quickly and tolerates frequent mowing as a living mulch between tomato rows; standard organic mid-Atlantic rotation.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide

entries.description: Cowpea (Vigna unguiculata) cover crop fixes nitrogen rapidly in summer heat; well-suited to southern double-cropping with sweet corn.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; UF/IFAS Extension

entries.description: Sunn hemp (Crotalaria juncea) summer cover fixes nitrogen in tropical and subtropical conditions; precedes fall-transplanted winter squash in long-season climates.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; UF/IFAS Extension

entries.description: Buckwheat strips alongside tomato draw hoverflies and parasitoid wasps that prey on aphids and tomato hornworm; bonus quick weed suppression.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Xerces Society

entries.description: Same mechanism as tomato, buckwheat strips support beneficial-insect populations that reduce pepper aphid and thrip pressure.
entries.source: SARE Cover Crops field guide; Xerces Society
